nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Polls every 15 min. Fires on the 1st of each month after 09:00 IST once the previous month is complete. Catches up on missed months the same way daily/weekly do.

nodes.name: Check if monthly digest is due

parameters.query:
SELECT
  (last_fetched_at AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata')::date::text AS period_start,
  ((last_fetched_at AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata')::date + INTERVAL '1 month')::date::text AS period_end
FROM sync_state
WHERE source = 'digest_monthly'
  AND NOW() >= (((last_fetched_at AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata')::date + INTERVAL '1 month')::timestamp + INTERVAL '9 hours') AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'

nodes.notes: last_fetched_at = 1st of next undigested month (midnight IST). Fires when we're past 9am IST on the 1st of the month AFTER that. Postgres INTERVAL '1 month' handles variable month lengths correctly.

parameters.query:
SELECT
  (SELECT count(*) FROM raw_events
   WHERE ingested_at >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND ingested_at < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata') AS raw_ingested,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM parsed_transactions
   WHERE parsed_at >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND parsed_at < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND ebk_status = 'written') AS written_to_ebk,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM parsed_transactions
   WHERE parsed_at >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND parsed_at < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND parse_method = 'groq') AS groq_calls,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM failed_events
   WHERE failed_at >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND failed_at < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND resolved = FALSE) AS open_failures,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM dedup_log
   WHERE first_seen_at >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND first_seen_at < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata') AS deduplicated,
  (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount),0) FROM parsed_transactions
   WHERE direction = 'debit'
     AND transaction_time >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND transaction_time < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata') AS month_spend,
  (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount),0) FROM parsed_transactions
   WHERE direction = 'credit'
     AND transaction_time >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
     AND transaction_time < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata') AS month_received

SELECT
  date_trunc('week', transaction_time AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata')::date::text AS week_start,
  (date_trunc('week', transaction_time AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata')::date + 6)::text AS week_end,
  count(*) AS transactions,
  COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN direction = 'debit' THEN amount END), 0) AS spent,
  COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN direction = 'credit' THEN amount END), 0) AS received
FROM parsed_transactions
WHERE transaction_time >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
  AND transaction_time < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
  AND ebk_status = 'written'
GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

SELECT
  COALESCE(pt.category_hint, 'uncategorized') AS category,
  count(*) AS transactions,
  COALESCE(SUM(pt.amount), 0) AS total
FROM parsed_transactions pt
WHERE pt.transaction_time >= $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
  AND pt.transaction_time < $2::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata'
  AND pt.direction = 'debit'
  AND pt.ebk_status = 'written'
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 3 DESC LIMIT 15

SELECT fe.id, fe.stage, fe.error_message, re.subject, re.sender, fe.failed_at FROM failed_events fe LEFT JOIN raw_events re ON fe.raw_event_id = re.id WHERE fe.resolved = FALSE ORDER BY fe.failed_at DESC LIMIT 10

nodes.name: Advance digest cursor

parameters.query: UPDATE sync_state SET last_fetched_at = $1::date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Kolkata', updated_at = NOW() WHERE source = 'digest_monthly'

nodes.notes: Advances last_fetched_at by exactly 1 month (to period_end). Next poll will check if another month is pending.

main.node: Check if monthly digest is due
